["fix(core): merge_content no longer mutates its first argument

merge_content bound `merged` to the first argument's list without copying,
then appended to / reassigned into it in place (base.py:399,405). It therefore
mutated (and returned) its first argument. Via BaseMessageChunk.__add__, this
meant `chunk_a + chunk_b` mutated chunk_a's content, breaking the immutability
contract of `+` and silently corrupting/double-counting content when a chunk is
reused as the left operand (e.g. streaming fold, retry, fan-out).

Copy list content before the in-place branches (strings are immutable)."]

['core: fix generations shape in stream/astream on_llm_error callback (GH#38276)

When BaseChatModel.stream()/astream() raises, on_llm_error was called with
a malformed LLMResult.generations:

- No chunks received → [[]] instead of []
- Partial chunks received → [[chunk], [error_meta]] (two outer rows for one
  prompt) instead of [[chunk + error_meta]]

Root cause: _generate_response_from_error() returns list[ChatGeneration];
the caller unconditionally wrapped it in another list, so an empty result
became [[]] and a partial result became [[chunk], []].

Fix: merge error metadata into the existing per-prompt row when chunks were
received; emit [] when neither chunks nor HTTP-error metadata are present;
only emit [error_meta_row] when an HTTP response with metadata exists and
no chunks were streamed.

Also remove the @pytest.mark.xfail from test_stream_error_callback since
the underlying bug is now fixed.']
['Handle Responses API structured parse fallback']

['fix(core): preserve non-ASCII in convert_to_openai_messages json blocks

`convert_to_openai_messages` serializes a `json` content block to text with
`json.dumps(block["json"])`, which defaults to `ensure_ascii=True` and
escapes non-ASCII characters (CJK, emoji, accents) to `\uXXXX`. The
`tool_use` path a few lines up already passes `ensure_ascii=False` (and has
a regression test asserting Unicode is preserved); the `json` block path is
the straggler, so the same message-conversion call escapes Unicode for one
block type but not the other.

Pass `ensure_ascii=False` to match, and extend
`test_convert_to_openai_messages_json` with a non-ASCII case mirroring the
existing `tool_use` Unicode assertions.']

['docs(langchain): add v1 examples suite and web playground

Add a self-contained, runnable examples suite for the actively maintained `langchain` v1 package, plus a small web playground that exercises it end to end.

- examples/01-04 + README: a ReAct tool agent, structured extraction via `response_format`, an in-memory chatbot, and a `TodoListMiddleware` planner — each a copy-paste starting point runnable with `uv run --with langchain-openai` (no manifest changes).
- examples/ui: a FastAPI backend wrapping the four agents and a React + Vite + Tailwind + shadcn/ui frontend (tabbed playground) that calls them via a dev-server proxy.

Verified: scripts run live against OpenAI; the frontend type-checks and builds; backend endpoints were exercised through the proxy. AI agent (Claude) assisted with this contribution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>']

['fix(core): stringify empty list tool output instead of treating as message content', 'fix(core): use `is not None` check for `llm_cache` in cache lookup functions

Caches implementing `__len__` evaluate as falsy when empty, causing
`get_prompts`, `aget_prompts`, and `aupdate_cache` to skip all cache
operations and raising a `KeyError` in `generate`/`agenerate`. Replace
truthiness checks with `is not None` to match the existing pattern in
`update_cache`.

Fixes #38440']
['docs(docs): add release note guidance to PR docs']

["fix(langchain): wrap deferred tools in a namespace for OpenAI tool search

`ProviderToolSearchMiddleware` emitted deferred tools as flat top-level
`function` specs carrying `defer_loading`. OpenAI's Responses API only
excludes deferred schemas from the billed input context when they are
nested in a `namespace` block, so the flat form still billed the full
schemas while the added `tool_search` tool became pure overhead, making
deferral cost more tokens instead of fewer.

Deferred tools are now serialized into flattened Responses-API `function`
specs and wrapped in a single `namespace` block for OpenAI. Anthropic
keeps its existing flat behavior, since its tool search uses a different
mechanism. Non-deferred tools pass through unchanged at the top level.

Closes #38544

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"]

["fix(agent): always include model in model_to_tools_destinations

The model_to_tools router can return 'model' (loop_entry_node) as a
destination when middleware injects synthetic ToolMessages (e.g. for
caching, HITL pre-approval, or replay), but the conditional edge's
path_map omitted it unless response_format was set or an after_model
middleware was present. This caused a KeyError('model') at runtime.

Fix by unconditionally adding loop_entry_node to the destinations list.

Fixes #38351", 'test(core): remove stale xfail markers for passing tests

Both test_convert_to_openai_function_nested_v2 and
test_sync_in_sync_lambdas now pass on master — the underlying
functionality (direct Pydantic v2 model support and sync-in-sync
streaming) was implemented in prior commits.

Removing the stale decorators cleans up XPASS in CI.

Fixes #38266']

["fix: file search rejects paths with '..' in directory names

_validate_and_resolve_path used `'..' in path` which rejected any
path whose directory name legitimately contained '..' as a substring
(e.g. '/src..old').  The check should only block path *segments*
that are exactly '..', which is what triggers traversal.

Switch to `any(part == '..' for part in path.split('/'))` so that
names like 'src..old' or 'v1..v2' are accepted, while actual
traversal attempts like '/foo/../etc' are still rejected.

The definitive security guard (`full_path.relative_to(root)`) is
unchanged and continues to catch any case that slips through.

Fixes #38549", 'fix(chroma): normalize scores in similarity_search_by_vector_with_relevance_scores

The method was returning raw Chroma distances (lower = more similar)
instead of normalized relevance scores in [0, 1] (higher = more similar).

Apply _select_relevance_score_fn() to each raw distance, matching the
behaviour documented in the docstring and expected by callers.
similarity_search_by_image_with_relevance_score is fixed transitively
because it delegates to this method.

Fixes #38506']

["docs(cookbook): add Uzbek low-resource RAG cookbook

Adds a cookbook notebook demonstrating culturally grounded RAG for Uzbek, a low-resource Central Asian language. Uses the public SOAS English-Uzbek RAG evaluation dataset (CC-BY-4.0).

Headline result: corpus supplementation improves Uzbek retrieval recall from 39% to 98% (Cohen's d = 2.91).

['fix(langchain-classic): skip POSIX chmod assertion on Windows

Windows does not enforce Unix-style group/other chmod permission bits,
so the strict octal assertion in `test_mset_chmod` always fails on
that platform. Skip the assertion on Windows while keeping it for
Unix platforms where `os.chmod` behaves as expected.

Closes #38329

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>']

['fix(langchain): reject only path components equal to `..`, not segments containing two dots

`_validate_and_resolve_path` previously used `".." in path` to
detect directory traversal, which also rejected valid path segments
that merely contain two consecutive dots (e.g. `src..old`,
`v1..backup`, `notes..archived`).

Switch to checking individual path components so only the literal
`..` segment is blocked. This matches the approach already used in
the glob pattern validation on line 186 of the same file.

Closes #38549

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>']

['fix(core): honor path boundaries in `extract_sub_links` scope check

When `prevent_outside=True`, `extract_sub_links` keeps only links that fall
under the base URL. The path check used a bare `str.startswith`, which also
matched siblings that merely share a string prefix: with a base URL of
`https://example.com/docs`, a link to `https://example.com/docsite/...` passed
even though it is not a child of `/docs`.

This contradicts the documented behavior of `prevent_outside` ("ignore
external links which are not children of the base URL") and lets crawls escape
their intended path subtree.

Require the base URL prefix to end on a path, query, or fragment boundary (or
to match exactly), so `/docs` no longer matches `/docsite` while genuine
descendants, the base URL itself, and the base URL followed by a query are
still kept. Domain-level scoping is unchanged (it is handled by the existing
`netloc` comparison).']

['fix(core): render falsy items in mustache list sections

When a mustache list section iterates over its items, each item is pushed
onto the scope stack and the section body is rendered for it. The body-skip
logic for falsy *sections* (e.g. `{{#x}}...{{/x}}` where `x` is falsy) keyed
off the truthiness of the current scope, so a falsy list *item* - `0`,
`False`, `0.0`, or `""` - was mistaken for a skipped section and its body
(including any static text) was silently dropped.

This made `PromptTemplate`/`ChatPromptTemplate` with `template_format="mustache"`
drop falsy entries: rendering `{{#scores}}({{.}}){{/scores}}` with
`scores=[0, 1, 2]` produced `(1)(2)` instead of `(0)(1)(2)`.

Mark a skipped section body with a dedicated sentinel scope instead of
overloading falsiness, so falsy list items are no longer confused with a
skipped section and render normally. All existing skip behavior (falsy
non-list sections, empty lists, inverted sections) is preserved.']

["fix(core): don't mask non-`Exception` `BaseException` in `agenerate`

`BaseChatModel.agenerate` gathers per-message results with
`return_exceptions=True` and collects failures with an
`isinstance(res, BaseException)` check, but the follow-up `on_llm_end`
step filtered results with the narrower `isinstance(res, Exception)`.

A `BaseException` that is not an `Exception` (e.g. `asyncio.CancelledError`
or `KeyboardInterrupt`) therefore slipped past the filter, its `generations`
attribute was read off the raised object, and the resulting `AttributeError`
masked the original exception.

Align the filter on `BaseException` so failed results are excluded
consistently and the original exception propagates. Narrowing the type also
makes the previous `type: ignore[union-attr]` comments unnecessary."]

["fix(openai): preserve OpenAI-compatible prompt manifest config

Add serialization support for OpenAI-compatible ChatOpenAI configs that use a
custom endpoint and a non-default API key secret name.

ChatOpenAI previously always serialized openai_api_key with the OPENAI_API_KEY
secret id, so prompt manifests generated from OpenAI-compatible provider
settings could not represent configs such as OpenRouter's OPENROUTER_API_KEY.

This change adds a serialization-only api_key_secret_id field and keeps the
default behavior as OPENAI_API_KEY. It also serializes custom endpoints with the
public base_url constructor alias instead of the internal openai_api_base field.

Adds a regression test in:
libs/partners/openai/tests/unit_tests/test_load.py"]

['fix(core): correct error message in `Visitor._validate_func` for disallowed operators

Closes #36701

The error message raised when an operator is not in `allowed_operators`
incorrectly said "Allowed comparators are ..." instead of
"Allowed operators are ...". This is a copy-paste error from the
adjacent comparator branch.

The fix changes a single word in the f-string so the message now
accurately reflects what is being validated, making it easier for
callers to distinguish operator failures from comparator failures.

A new unit test file covers both the fixed path (operator error no
longer leaks the word "comparators") and the existing comparator path,
as well as the happy-path cases that must not raise at all.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>', 'fix(qdrant): add missing `await` in `asimilarity_search` docstring example

Closes #37058

The async usage example in the `QdrantVectorStore` class docstring
was missing `await` on the `asimilarity_search` call:

    # results = vector_store.asimilarity_search(query="thud", k=1)

Without `await`, this returns a coroutine object instead of the
expected list of `Document` objects. Every other async example in the
same docstring block correctly uses `await`, making this inconsistent
and confusing for users copying the example.

Also normalized spacing: added a space after the comma in `k=1`
to match the style of the surrounding async examples.

> Note: this contribution was prepared with the assistance of Claude Code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>', 'fix(core,chroma): add missing `await` in `asimilarity_search` docstring examples

The async usage sections in both `InMemoryVectorStore` and
`Chroma` class docstrings were missing `await` on the
`asimilarity_search` call:

    # results = vector_store.asimilarity_search(query="thud", k=1)

Without `await`, this expression yields a coroutine object rather
than a `list[Document]`. Every surrounding async example in both
docstrings already uses `await` correctly (`aadd_documents`,

Also normalised the spacing after the comma in the Chroma example

The identical issue in `langchain-qdrant` was fixed separately
in PR #38539.

> Note: this contribution was prepared with the assistance of Claude Code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>', "fix(core): clarify that JSON schema dicts require a top-level `title` key

Closes #34662

Users passing a raw JSON schema dict to `convert_to_openai_function`
or `convert_to_openai_tool` frequently hit a `ValueError` whose
message mentions the requirement, but the docstrings for both functions
do not explain it upfront.

The existing text only says 'a JSON schema with top-level `title` key
specified' in passing within the `Args` description, without making
clear that this key is **required** (not optional) when using a dict,
or explaining what it is used for (the function/tool name).

This commit expands both docstrings to:
- Explicitly state that `title` is **required** for dict inputs
- Explain that its value becomes the function/tool name
- Provide a minimal, copy-pasteable example showing the correct shape
- Add the raised `ValueError` to the `Raises` section of
  `convert_to_openai_function`

No behaviour is changed.

> Note: this contribution was prepared with the assistance of Claude Code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>", 'fix(core,text-splitters): fix InMemoryCache eviction on update and add Language.PERL separators

Closes #36750

Two distinct bugs fixed:

**Bug 1 — InMemoryCache incorrectly evicts on update (langchain-core)**

`InMemoryCache.update()` checked `len(cache) == maxsize` and evicted
the oldest entry unconditionally, even when the caller was overwriting
an existing key (not inserting a new one). This shrinks the cache below
`maxsize` on every re-update of an existing entry.

Fix: only evict when the key is not already present in the cache.

**Bug 2 — Language.PERL not handled in get_separators_for_language (text-splitters)**

`Language.PERL` exists in the `Language` enum but the
`get_separators_for_language` switch had no branch for it, causing a
`ValueError: Language Language.PERL is not implemented yet!` on every
call to `RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter.from_language(Language.PERL)`.

Fix: added a PERL branch with idiomatic Perl separators:
sub/package/use/require declarations, eval blocks, control-flow
keywords (if/elsif/else/unless/while/for/foreach/until/do), regex
operators (=~ s/ and =~ m/), and the standard fallback sequence.

> Note: this contribution was prepared with the assistance of Claude Code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>']

['fix(anthropic): read source path from `args["path"]` in file rename handlers

The `rename` command of the state- and filesystem-backed text editor and
memory middleware always raised `KeyError: 'old_path'`. The tool builds
the handler `args` with the source path under the `path` key (and the
destination under `new_path`) and never populates `old_path`, but both
`_handle_rename` methods read `args["old_path"]`. Every sibling handler
(`_handle_view`, `_handle_create`, `_handle_str_replace`, `_handle_insert`,
`_handle_delete`) correctly reads `args["path"]`.

Read the source path from `args["path"]` in both `_handle_rename` methods so
`rename` works end-to-end.

['fix(agents): include loop_entry_node in model_to_tools destinations unconditionally

The router (_make_model_to_tools_edge) can return model_destination
(loop_entry_node) on the "artificial tool messages" branch in EVERY
configuration, not just when response_format or after_model are present.

A wrap_model_call middleware that injects synthetic ToolMessages produces
this scenario (documented pattern for caching, HITL pre-approval, and
replay). Previously, the path_map omitted 'model' in the default
configuration, causing KeyError('model') from langgraph's branch
dispatcher.

Fixes langchain-ai/langchain#38351', 'fix: remove orphan ToolMessages after summarization cutoff

When _find_safe_cutoff_point moves the cutoff backward to include a
matching AIMessage, consecutive orphan ToolMessages (those without a
preceding AIMessage containing the matching tool_call_id) could also
end up in the preserved section. Sending these orphans to the provider
causes a 400 error because no prior AIMessage requested that tool call.

Add _filter_orphan_tool_messages() to strip ToolMessages whose
tool_call_id has no corresponding AIMessage in the preserved section.
Apply it in both before_model and abefore_model after partitioning.

Fixes #38352

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>']

["fix(core): prevent merge_dicts from concatenating identical metadata strings

When streaming chunks from providers like OpenRouter that send multiple
terminal chunks with identical metadata (model_name, finish_reason, etc.),
merge_dicts was concatenating these identical strings instead of preserving
them. For example, 'model_name: gpt-4' + 'model_name: gpt-4' would become
'model_name: gpt-4gpt-4'.

This fix extends the existing deduplication guard (previously only for
'id', 'output_version', 'model_provider') to also cover:
- model_name
- finish_reason
- native_finish_reason
- object
- system_fingerprint
- format

Fixes #38366"]

['fix(anthropic): preserve cache_control on tool_use blocks when tool_calls exist

When an AIMessage has a tool_use content block with cache_control AND a
matching tool_calls entry, _format_messages rebuilds the block from
tool_calls (the preferred source). Previously this discarded cache_control
along with all other extra keys. Now cache_control from the original block
is forwarded to _lc_tool_calls_to_anthropic_tool_use_blocks and applied
to the rebuilt blocks.

Fixes #38398

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>']

['fix(core): preserve bool metadata in merge_dicts

['fix(core): deduplicate identical string values in `merge_dicts` to prevent metadata doubling during streaming

Streaming providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) repeat metadata fields such
as `model_name` and `finish_reason` with the same value in every chunk.
`merge_dicts` was concatenating those strings, producing doubled values like
`"gpt-4gpt-4"` or `"stopstop"` in the aggregated message.

Replace the narrow allowlist (`id`, `output_version`, `model_provider`) with
a general check: if two identical string values are merged, skip concatenation.
Different values (sequential content fragments like `"He"` + `"llo"`) are
still concatenated as before.

Fixes #38366

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>']

["fix(langchain): prevent orphan ToolMessages in SummarizationMiddleware

_find_safe_cutoff_point used set intersection to detect a matching AIMessage
when the cutoff landed on a ToolMessage run. In conversations where one
tool_call_id in the run belongs to a surviving AIMessage while another belongs
to an AIMessage that was already summarized away, the intersection matched
prematurely and left the mismatched ToolMessage as an orphan in the preserved
slice. On the next LLM call providers reject the input with a 400 error
(OpenAI: 'tool must be a response to a preceding message with tool_calls';
Anthropic: 'tool_use_id has no matching tool_result').

The fix accumulates remaining unmatched ids as we walk backward and only
places the cutoff once every id in the cutoff run is fully covered by
AIMessages encountered so far. If coverage is never complete we fall through
to the existing advance-forward fallback, which is the safer choice.

Fixes #38352", 'fix(langchain-core): normalize on_llm_error generations shape in stream/astream

BaseChatModel.stream() and astream() passed a malformed generations value to
on_llm_error when the stream raised an exception. LLMResult.generations is
typed list[list[Generation]] where the outer dimension is per-prompt (batch).
A single-prompt stream must produce exactly one outer row.

Two incorrect shapes were emitted:

1. Error before any output (i == 0): emitted [[]]. Every other on_llm_error
   call site in langchain-core emits [] for this scenario.
2. Partial output then error (i > 0): emitted [[chunk], []] -- two outer rows
   for a single prompt. The error-metadata flat list was placed as a second
   batch row instead of being merged into the same inner row as the chunk.

Root cause: _generate_response_from_error returns a flat list[ChatGeneration]
and returns [] when the exception carries no .response attribute. The callers
wrapped it unconditionally as [generations_with_error_metadata], turning an
empty result into [[]] and appending it as a second outer row when chunks
were present.

Fix: merge the partial chunk and error metadata into a single inner list
(one outer row) and pass [] when both are empty, matching every other
on_llm_error call site.

Removes the xfail marker from test_stream_error_callback; the test was
correctly documenting the expected behavior.

Fixes #38276']

["fix(langchain): prevent orphan ToolMessages in SummarizationMiddleware

_find_safe_cutoff_point used set intersection to detect a matching AIMessage
when the cutoff landed on a ToolMessage run. In conversations where one
tool_call_id in the run belongs to a surviving AIMessage while another belongs
to an AIMessage that was already summarized away, the intersection matched
prematurely and left the mismatched ToolMessage as an orphan in the preserved
slice. On the next LLM call providers reject the input with a 400 error
(OpenAI: 'tool must be a response to a preceding message with tool_calls';
Anthropic: 'tool_use_id has no matching tool_result').

The fix accumulates remaining unmatched ids as we walk backward and only
places the cutoff once every id in the cutoff run is fully covered by
AIMessages encountered so far. If coverage is never complete we fall through
to the existing advance-forward fallback, which is the safer choice.

Fixes #38352"]

["fix(langchain): always include loop_entry_node in model-to-tools edge destinations

_make_model_to_tools_edge returns model_destination on the 'all pending tool
calls already answered' branch regardless of whether response_format or
after_model middleware are present. create_agent previously only added
loop_entry_node to the conditional-edge path_map when response_format was
set or loop_exit_node was not 'model'. When wrap_model_call middleware injects
synthetic ToolMessages (caching, HITL pre-approval, replay) the router picks
model_destination but it is absent from the path_map, causing LangGraph's
branch dispatcher to raise KeyError('model').

The fix unconditionally includes loop_entry_node in model_to_tools_destinations.
Existing configurations are unaffected: the extra destination is simply a
no-op when the router never selects it.

Fixes #38351"]

['fix(langchain-classic): skip test_mset_chmod on Windows

Unix-style group/other permission bits are silently ignored by the Windows
filesystem. The LocalFileStore implementation is correct; only the test
assertion is non-portable. Adding a skipif guard avoids spurious failures in
Windows CI without touching production code.

Fixes #38329']

['fix(anthropic): preserve cache_control on tool_use blocks in _format_messages

When an AIMessage carries a tool_use block with cache_control and a
matching entry in tool_calls, _format_messages reconstructed the
tool_use block from the structured tool_calls data and silently dropped
every extra key on the original block, including cache_control. This
caused prompt-cache breakpoints placed on tool_use blocks to be lost
during message formatting.

The text, thinking, server_tool_use, and mcp_tool_use branches already
preserved cache_control; tool_use was the only type that dropped it.

Fix: copy cache_control from the original content block onto the
reconstructed tool_use block in both the overlapping-ID path (where
tool_calls takes precedence) and the inline tool_use path.

Fixes #38398']

['fix(core): extend merge_dicts dedup to stable response metadata fields

When providers send multiple terminal chunks with identical metadata
values (finish_reason, model_name, object, etc.), merge_dicts
concatenates the strings instead of deduplicating them, producing
values like "stopstop" or doubled model names.

The existing allowlist already handles id, output_version, and
model_provider with an equal-value guard. Extend that same guard to
model_name, finish_reason, native_finish_reason, object,
system_fingerprint, and format — all fields that providers repeat
verbatim across terminal chunks.

Values that genuinely differ across chunks still concatenate as
before; the guard only skips when merged[key] == incoming value.

Fixes #38366']

['fix(core): treat empty caches as present in prompt-cache helpers

`get_prompts`, `aget_prompts` and `aupdate_cache` decided whether a cache
was configured with a truthiness check (`if llm_cache:`) rather than an
identity check. A `BaseCache` that implements `__len__` (common, to report
its entry count) is falsy when empty, so the lookup loop in
`get_prompts`/`aget_prompts` was skipped entirely: the prompt was added to
neither `existing_prompts` nor `missing_prompts`, generation was skipped,
and `generate`/`agenerate` raised `KeyError` building the result.

Use `is not None` in all three places, matching the sync `update_cache`,
so an empty-but-present cache is treated the same as a populated one.

Adds regression tests covering a cache that implements `__len__`.']

["fix(core): don't clobber tool args named like the config param (#34029)", 'test(core): cover tool arg named config not clobbered (#34029)', "fix(core): forward tool args named like the config param instead of clobbering them (#34029)

StructuredTool._run/_arun reserve a keyword-only `config: RunnableConfig`
parameter. A tool whose own function declares an argument named `config`
had that value bound to the wrapper's reserved param and never forwarded,
raising 'missing 1 required positional argument: config'.

- base.py: don't overwrite a user-supplied tool arg with the injected
  RunnableConfig when the names collide.
- structured.py: forward the captured value to the underlying function/
  coroutine when it declares a (non-RunnableConfig) parameter named config.
- tests: cover the collision (sync + async) and a regression guard that
  genuine RunnableConfig params are still injected."]

['fix(openai): reuse cached httpx clients in AzureChatOpenAI (#32489)

AzureChatOpenAI was passing http_client=None to openai.AzureOpenAI()
and openai.AsyncAzureOpenAI() on every instantiation, causing the
underlying SDK to create a brand-new httpx client each time. This
wastes TCP connections and prevents connection pooling.

The base ChatOpenAI class already solved this via the cached helpers
_get_default_httpx_client / _get_default_async_httpx_client (backed
by @lru_cache), but AzureChatOpenAI had its own validate_environment
that bypassed them.

This commit aligns AzureChatOpenAI with BaseChatOpenAI by:
- Importing and using _get_default_httpx_client / _get_default_async_httpx_client
- Resolving socket options via _resolve_socket_options
- Handling proxy env bypass via _should_bypass_socket_options_for_proxy_env
- Supporting openai_proxy via _build_proxied_sync/async_httpx_client

Fixes langchain-ai/langchain#32489']

['fix: adapt test_mset_chmod for platforms with limited chmod support

On Windows, os.chmod silently ignores group and other permission bits
(e.g., chmod(0o770) results in 0o777 on disk). This caused
test_mset_chmod[770-660] and test_mset_chmod[700-600] to fail with:
    AssertionError: assert (16895 & 511) == 504

This fix adds a runtime _is_chmod_fully_supported() check instead of
blindly skipping the test on Windows. When full chmod is not available
(e.g. Windows), only the owner permission bits (0o700) are asserted,
preserving test coverage for the owner bits while accepting platform
limitations for group/other bits.

Fixes #38329']

['docs(evaluation): recommend gpt-4o-mini in eval chain examples

The Example blocks in ScoreStringEvalChain and PairwiseStringEvalChain
docstrings instantiate `ChatOpenAI(model_name="gpt-4")`. Copy-pasting
these examples sends evaluation traffic to the gpt-4 endpoint, which is
roughly an order of magnitude more expensive than gpt-4o-mini on both
input and output tokens for equal or better quality on most short
scoring / pairwise comparison tasks.

This change updates only the docstring examples. It does not change any
runtime behavior:

- The `from_llm(...)` warning gate uses
  `llm.model_name.startswith("gpt-4")`, which still matches gpt-4o-mini
  (and gpt-4o, gpt-4-turbo, etc.), so the example continues to run
  warning-free.
- No public API, type, or default value is changed.

A short comment is added above the example explaining why gpt-4o-mini
is the recommended default and which model names skip the
"untested model" warning.']

["fix(core): handle partials in `pretty_repr`

`StringPromptTemplate.pretty_repr` only built placeholders for
`input_variables`, so any `partial_variables` were filled with their bound
values (or the return value of callable partials) instead of being shown as
template placeholders. This also fixed the `HumanMessagePromptTemplate` and
`ChatMessagePromptTemplate` cases, which delegate to the inner prompt's
`pretty_repr`.

Partial variable names are now rendered as placeholders as well, and the
`# TODO: handle partials` is removed. Adds regression tests covering partials
applied via `.partial()`, via the constructor, and callable partials."]

['fix(core): remove stale xfail markers from tests

Closes #38266

---

Two tests in langchain-core were marked with xfail but now pass:

- 	est_convert_to_openai_function_nested_v2 — pydantic v2 nested models
  are now supported by convert_to_openai_function
- 	est_sync_in_sync_lambdas — the sync-in-sync streaming path now
  emits events correctly

Removing the markers eliminates the XPASS noise in the test suite.', "fix(core): raise ValueError for mismatched ids in VectorStore.add_texts

Closes #38203

---

VectorStore.add_texts silently truncates texts when len(ids) < len(texts)
because zip with strict=False stops at the shortest iterable. This causes
data loss without any error.

This adds validation to raise ValueError when the number of ids doesn't
match the number of texts, consistent with the existing validation for
metadatas and the documented contract in the docstring.", 'fix(core): apply fixed token penalty for audio/video/file blocks

Closes #38229

image/image_url blocks, but other multimodal data blocks (audio, video,
file) fell through to repr(block), causing their base64 payloads to be
char-counted. This resulted in massive token overestimation for non-image
multimodal content.

This adds the same fixed token penalty for audio, video, and file blocks,
consistent with how image blocks are handled.', 'perf(core): fix O(n²) scaling in merge_message_runs for list content

Closes #38217

---

merge_message_runs scaled quadratically with the length of a run of
consecutive same-type messages when those messages used list (content-block)
content. This was because it merged messages pairwise, rebuilding the
accumulated message on every step with Pydantic re-validation.

This fix collects all messages in a run first, then merges them in one
go, reducing the complexity from O(n²) to O(n).']

['Guard the shell-error callbacks instead of nulling them out on the request

The previous change stopped `onAllReady` from firing after a fatal shell error
by assigning `noop` to `request.onAllReady` inside `fatalError`. This mutated a
completion callback on the request as a side channel, mirroring the existing
`request.onShellError = noop` in `completeShell`. This refactor removes both
noop assignments and instead guards the calls at the point where they would
happen, so the callbacks on the request stay the ones the caller passed in.

For `onAllReady`, `erroredTask` now returns right after `fatalError` in the root
(`boundary === null`) case, so it no longer falls through to the
`allPendingTasks === 0` check that calls `completeAll`. This matches the sibling
`finishAbortedTask`, which already returns after a fatal root error. Later task
completions cannot reach `completeAll` either, because `performWork` bails out
once the request status is past `OPEN`.

For `onShellError`, `fatalError` now decides whether to call it based on the
same condition that governed the assignment: the shell is complete exactly when
`request.pendingRootTasks === 0`, and `completeShell` only runs at that point,
so guarding the call is equivalent to the previous nulling out. `onFatalError`
still always fires because the error is always fatal to the request.

This is a behavior-preserving refactor of the fix landed in #36903; the tests
added there continue to pass unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>']

["Add regression test for hydrating a defer <script> in the <head>

This adds a test to the ReactDOMFizzStatic suite covering the scenario
reported in https://github.com/react/react/issues/36169, where a
non-async <script> with `defer` rendered inside the <head> of a fully
prerendered document was reported to trigger a hydration mismatch. The
test prerenders such a document with `prerenderToNodeStream`, loads the
prelude into the DOM the same way the issue does (`document.write`), and
then hydrates the whole document, asserting that no recoverable errors
are reported.

While investigating we could not reproduce the mismatch in React's own
JSDOM-based test environment on either the stable or experimental
channel: the `<script>` hydrates cleanly. The original report used
happy-dom, whose parser injects an extra text node into the `<script>`
element, which React then correctly flags as a mismatch. The test
therefore documents and guards React's correct behavior here rather than
reproducing the environment-specific failure.

["[Perf Tracks] Don't enumerate typed array props in dev

Passing a large TypedArray (e.g. `new Float32Array(25000000)`) as a prop
froze rendering in development since 19.2. The performance track property
logger recursed into the value and enumerated it with `for...in`, which
forces the engine to materialize a key for every index up front (~2s+ for
25M elements) even though the loop breaks after 100 entries.

Skip enumeration for typed arrays and show the type and length instead
(e.g. `Float32Array(25000000)`), which is both fast and more useful.
`ArrayBuffer` and `DataView` don't have enumerable indexed properties, so
they were never affected.

Fixes #36200

['Fix: Treat incomplete tree as an error during recovery

In `recoverFromConcurrentError`, there's logic to determine if the latest render
attempt resulted in another error or if it completed successfully, by checking
whether the exit status matches RootErrored.

The logic was incomplete because RootSuspendedAtTheShell is also considered an
errored state in this context. This can cause an incomplete tree (i.e. one that
unwinds without entering the complete phase) to be mistaken for a complete one,
leading to confusing errors. An incomplete tree can never be committed because
it's not guaranteed to represent a coherent state.

An example of how this can manifest as a bug: in #33580, an error caught by an
error boundary triggers a synchronous recovery render. During that render a
parent component suspends on `use(thenable)` with no Suspense boundary above it,
so the tree unwinds to the shell instead of completing. Because the incomplete
tree is mistaken for a recovered one and committed, the parent fiber becomes
current with a truncated hook list — only the hooks it rendered before
suspending. On the next render it calls the rest of its hooks and throws
"Rendered more hooks than during the previous render."

Fixes #33580.

Co-authored-by: Arunanshu Biswas <48434243+arunanshub@users.noreply.github.com>']

['Add tests documenting onAllReady behavior on shell and boundary errors

These tests exercise the public `renderToPipeableStream` callbacks to pin
down when `onAllReady` fires relative to the error callbacks. When an error
is thrown at the root, the shell fatally errors and `onShellError` fires;
the render never completes, yet `onAllReady` is still invoked once. This is
the current behavior and is the bug tracked in facebook/react#36890.

The second test covers the contrasting documented case: when the error is
contained by a Suspense boundary, the shell stays valid and the boundary is
client-rendered, so the render completes and `onAllReady` fires as expected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>', "Stop firing onAllReady after a fatal shell error

When the shell fatally errors, `fatalError` reports the failure through
`onShellError` and `onFatalError`, but the request's pending task count can
still reach zero afterwards and trigger `completeAll`, which invokes
`onAllReady`. That signals a successful, fully ready render to consumers even
though nothing was rendered, as reported in facebook/react#36890.

This change neutralizes `onAllReady` inside `fatalError` by replacing it with
`noop`, mirroring how `completeShell` already clears `onShellError` once the
shell can no longer error. A fatal shell error is now terminal: `onAllReady`
no longer fires after it. The contrasting boundary-error case is unaffected,
because that path completes the shell and is expected to fire `onAllReady`.

The test added in the previous commit is updated to assert that `onAllReady`
is not called after a fatal shell error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>"]

['[compiler] Restore code frames in ESLint compiler error messages

Restore the per-detail printing in both ESLint integrations by exporting
printCodeFrame from CompilerError and reusing it. The detail loop handles
both detail shapes that flow through LoggerEvents: a `details` array
(CompilerDiagnostic and the Rust compiler) and a legacy flat `loc`
(deprecated CompilerErrorDetail), so it works with the Rust compiler as

['[Fizz] Reduce chunk push overhead in HTML serialization

Batch attribute and style serialization into single string pushes
instead of 3-5 separate push calls per attribute. This reduces array
operations, chunk count in segment buffers, and downstream
Buffer.byteLength and writeChunk/encodeInto call volume.

Changes:
- pushStringAttribute: 5 pushes → 1 concatenated string push
- pushBooleanAttribute: 3 pushes → 1 push
- pushStyleAttribute: 4N+1 pushes → 1 push (build full style string)
- pushAttribute default/URL/enumerated/numeric cases: 5 pushes → 1 push
- processStyleName: returns plain string instead of PrecomputedChunk
- pushStartGenericElement/pushStartAnchor: merge close tag with text
  children into single push when no dangerouslySetInnerHTML
- byteLengthOfChunk: use string.length instead of Buffer.byteLength
  for heuristic byte sizing (outlining threshold, progressive chunks).
  For ASCII (99%+ of HTML output), length === byte length.

Benchmark (Node v24, Apple M1 Max, 1000 iterations, 100 warmup):

| Tree                        | Before    | After     | Δ        |
|-----------------------------|-----------|-----------|----------|
| Small (10 el)               | 37,959    | 48,257    | +27.1%   |
| Medium (300 el, Suspense)   | 2,309     | 3,518     | +52.4%   |
| Medium (300 el, no Suspense)| 2,404     | 3,725     | +54.9%   |
| Large (2500+ el, Suspense)  | 429       | 626       | +45.9%   |', '[Fizz] Batch attribute and style serialization into single pushes

Reduces target.push() call volume in HTML serialization by building
attribute, boolean, style, and text-child output as single strings.
Excludes the byteLengthOfChunk fast path, which is unsound for
multi-byte content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>', '[Fizz] Add attribute-batching benchmark

Renders 1000 attribute-heavy elements through renderToPipeableStream.
Paired interleaved runs vs main: ~275 -> ~452 ops/s (~+64%).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>']

["[compiler] Fix over-eager scope merging for intermediate function call results (#36848)

When a function call result is passed as an argument to another call
(e.g. `intermediate = cheap(); return expensive(intermediate)`), the
compiler was merging both calls into a single reactive scope, causing
`expensive` to re-run whenever `cheap`'s identity changed even if
`intermediate`'s value was unchanged.

The fix has three parts: (1) extend a call result's mutable range
through its StoreLocal (but not through subsequent LoadLocals, which
would pull argument-loading instructions inside the wrong scope body);
(2) stop backward alias propagation of conditional mutations during real
range extension, preventing conservative MutateTransitiveConditionally
effects from inflating upstream scope ranges; (3) require definite
Mutate/MutateTransitive effects before unioning a non-allocating lvalue
or a CallExpression operand into a scope, so temporaries created only
to pass values as arguments don't join the callee's scope.", "Merge branch 'main' into issue#36848-parva"]

["[scripts] Fix missing VERSION_NATIVE_FB in commit artifacts

The runtime_commit_artifacts workflow was failing with:

  mv: cannot stat 'build/facebook-react-native/VERSION_NATIVE_FB': No such file or directory

VERSION_NATIVE_FB was only written in processExperimental, inside
build/facebook-react-native/. That directory is not in the allowlist of
the cleanup loop at the end of processExperimental, so the experimental
workers wrote the version file and then immediately deleted it.

This was masked until #36859: the cleanup loop previously used
`rm -rm` (an invalid flag), which errored out and deleted nothing. Once
that was changed to fs.rmSync the deletion actually took effect, and the
merged CI artifact ended up with facebook-react-native/*/cjs (from the
stable workers, which have no such cleanup) but no VERSION_NATIVE_FB.

The facebook-react-native artifacts are identical across channels (RN
packages use the generic .fb.js entry fork, and the native-fb feature
flag forks don't reference __EXPERIMENTAL__), so write VERSION_NATIVE_FB
from processStable where the directory is preserved, and drop the dead
write from processExperimental."]

['Test sync use microtask suspension behavior

Add coverage for the behavior we want before changing the work loop: a normal sync render should not show a Suspense fallback when a cached promise resolves in the next microtask.

Also cover the boundaries: if the promise is still pending after the microtask, React should commit the fallback, and flushSync should keep its current immediate fallback behavior.', 'Replay or unwind suspended sync render

When sync render resumes from a suspended promise, it now makes the same choice as concurrent render: retry the suspended fiber if the promise resolved, or unwind if it is still pending.

Add the SuspendedAndReadyToContinue path to renderRootSync and share that logic with renderRootConcurrent. This commit only adds the resume path. The next commit will make non-flushSync sync renders wait one microtask before using it.', 'Pause scheduled sync render before unwinding

Scheduled sync work can now stop before unwinding an immediate suspend inside a Suspense boundary. This gives promise microtasks a chance to resolve before React re-enters the work loop, so the render can replay instead of immediately showing the fallback.

Explicit and forced sync work stay on the immediate unwind path.', 'Preserve sync unwind after paused suspend

When React resumes a paused sync render, the thenable may still be pending. In that case, keep unwinding the suspended fiber as an immediate suspend.

This keeps the existing sync fallback/prewarming path for unresolved thenables.', 'Check external stores after sync promise replay

Scheduled sync renders can now pause before unwinding a Suspense fallback so promise microtasks can resolve first. That gives the render a chance to replay, but it also means an external store may change after a component has already read its snapshot.

Track the existing StoreConsistency checks for sync renders that may pause for a promise microtask, and run the same pre-commit check that concurrent renders use if the sync render actually paused. If a snapshot is stale, restart the render synchronously without pausing again.

Add a regression test where a store updates during the microtask pause.', 'Keep ReactMemo lazy fixture pending past replay

ReactMemo-test expects the lazy fallback to render. Make the fake import resolve after the sync render microtask pause, so it still behaves like an import that has not already loaded.', 'Update DevTools profiling snapshot for sync replay

Sync render can now replay a suspended fiber after an immediately resolved promise settles in a microtask. This profiling test uses that shape: Async first renders for 3ms and suspends, then renders for another 3ms with the resolved promise before the commit.

The profiler reports both render attempts in that commit, so the snapshot now expects 6ms.']

["[Fiber] Clarify shell-suspend error for use() of an uncached promise

Broaden the message to name both causes and point at the fixes (cache the

Fixes #30709"]

['[Transition] Keep previous UI when a popstate transition suspends

A transition flushed synchronously during a popstate event carries a
synthetic SyncLane (added by getNextLanesToFlushSync to mark the batch
priority). When such a transition suspended, the extra lane made
finishConcurrentRender treat it as a non-transition update and commit the
Suspense fallback, hiding already-revealed content. Ignore the synthetic
SyncLane when the suspended work is otherwise a popstate transition so it
falls back to concurrent rendering and keeps the previous UI on screen.

Fixes #35966']

['[Flight] Test current behavior for serializing a Node Buffer', '[Flight] Tailor the warning for binary data with a toJSON (Node Buffer)

A Node.js `Buffer` carries a `toJSON` method, so Flight serializes it
through that method instead of as binary, and it is deserialized as a
plain `{type: 'Buffer', data: [...]}` object. The existing warning
reported this as "Uint8Array objects are not supported", which is
misleading on two counts: the value is a `Buffer`, reported as
`Uint8Array` only because that is its `Object.prototype.toString` tag,
and a real `Uint8Array` is in fact supported, since it has no `toJSON`
and serializes as binary. For values that are `ArrayBuffer.isView`, this
replaces that message with one that names the actual cause and the fix:
the data is serialized through `toJSON` instead of as binary, so pass a
`Uint8Array` or `ArrayBuffer` to send binary data.']

['Guard prop getter reads in DEV perf logger

In dev mode, logComponentRender walks every prop object via for...in,
calling obj[key] directly. A user-defined getter can throw or have
side effects, which then escapes the commit phase and corrupts the
fiber tree.

Wrap the property read in a try/catch and substitute the omitted-prop
ellipsis on failure so the perf logger degrades gracefully instead of
breaking the app.

This is complementary to #36829 (which guards the `in` operator and
the $$typeof probe) — that PR is necessary for cross-origin Windows;
this one covers any prop whose own getter throws (issue #35126).

Fixes #35126']

['Forward fetchPriority option in ReactDOM.preloadModule

ReactDOM.preloadModule() ignored it. Add fetchPriority to

['Harden Server Reference resolution in the FB Flight bundler

ReactFlightClientConfigBundlerFB.js was missing the Server Reference
resolution guards the other bundlers received in #35277. When a reply is
decoded on the server, a request-controlled reference id could resolve to an
arbitrary module export.

- resolveServerReference is the only place Server References are resolved
  (client references go through resolveClientReference). On the server it now
  requires the module and rejects any id whose resolved export was not
  registered via registerServerReference. This bundler has no manifest to
  allowlist against, so the registration tag is the equivalent check.

- requireModule reads only the module's own exports via hasOwnProperty,
  preventing ids like "x#constructor" from reaching prototype-chain
  properties, matching the other bundlers.

Test plan:
- yarn prettier and yarn eslint clean on the changed file
- yarn flow dom-node: No errors']

['[compiler] Treat hook calls as reactive scope barriers

Fixes #36807. A conservatively-mutable value created before a hook call
and read by a ref callback was dragged into a reactive scope spanning the
hook, which FlattenScopesWithHooksOrUse prunes wholesale, de-memoizing the
ref callback (unstable ref identity every render).

InferReactiveScopeVariables no longer unions operands across a hook
barrier, and MergeOverlappingReactiveScopes no longer re-merges scopes
across a hook for non-mutating references. Reads stay independently
memoized; genuine captures still merge.', "Merge branch 'main' into fix-36807-ref-callback-hook-scope", "Merge branch 'main' into fix-36807-ref-callback-hook-scope", '[compiler] Correct linked issue reference in regression fixture (#36800)', "Merge branch 'main' into fix-36807-ref-callback-hook-scope", "Merge branch 'main' into fix-36807-ref-callback-hook-scope", "Merge branch 'main' into fix-36807-ref-callback-hook-scope"]

['feat: add fetchPriority support to preloadModule() and preinitModule()

ReactDOM.preload() and ReactDOM.preinit() already accept a fetchPriority
option, but the module variants (preloadModule/preinitModule) were missing
it. This adds fetchPriority to both the public API types and the internal
implementation types, and forwards it through the dispatcher calls.

The server/client implementations already use Object.assign to spread
options onto the emitted element props, so no changes are needed there.

Closes #36834']

["[react-devtools] Fix trailing percent sign in formatConsoleArguments

`formatConsoleArguments` inlines `%s`/`%d`/`%i`/`%f` substitutions into the
first console argument while leaving `%c`/`%o`/`%O` in place. When a format
string ends with a lone `%` (e.g. `console.log('Progress 100%', value)`),
the loop reads the character after `%`, which is `undefined`, and the
`default` branch appended `` `%${nextChar}` `` — producing the literal text
`%undefined` in the output.

Browsers treat a trailing `%` as a literal percent sign, so keep it as `%`
when there is no following character.", "Merge branch 'main' into devtools-format-console-trailing-percent"]

["[react-dom] Forward nonce option in ReactDOM.preloadModule()

`ReactDOM.preloadModule()` accepts a `nonce` option in its public type
(`PreloadModuleOptions`), and React already emits `nonce` on the
`modulepreload` links it generates for bootstrap modules. However, the
public `preloadModule()` API silently dropped the option: it only
forwarded `as`, `crossOrigin`, and `integrity` to the host dispatcher.

As a result, a `modulepreload` link requested with a nonce was emitted
without one, so under a strict CSP (`script-src 'nonce-...'`) the browser
blocks the preload. Both `ReactDOM.preload()` and
`ReactDOM.preinitModule()` already forward `nonce`, so `preloadModule()`
was the odd one out.

Forward `nonce` to the dispatcher. No type change is needed since `nonce`
is already declared on `PreloadModuleOptions` and
`PreloadModuleImplOptions`, and the client, Fizz, and Flight paths
already spread the impl options onto the generated link / hint.", "[Float] Require options on host dispatcher methods

Per review, type the option argument of the host dispatcher's preload,
preloadModule, preinitStyle, preinitScript, and preinitModuleScript methods
as Required<...>. This forces the public API to forward every option to the
dispatcher instead of silently dropping ones it forgets to spread (the class
of bug this PR's nonce fix addressed).

Object literals passed to these methods must now list every key; forwarded
option variables remain compatible since the maybe-typed fields read as
undefined when absent. This also surfaced two SSR helpers
(preinitModuleForSSR, preinitScriptForSSR) that weren't forwarding all
options.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>", "Merge branch 'main' into preloadmodule-nonce"]

['[react-dom] Support fetchPriority in ReactDOM.preloadModule()

`ReactDOM.preload()` and `ReactDOM.preinit()` accept a `fetchPriority`
option, but `ReactDOM.preloadModule()` silently dropped it, so there was
no way to lower the priority of the `modulepreload` links emitted for
native ESM apps. This matters when a page emits many module scripts that
would otherwise compete with render-critical resources.

The attribute itself is already supported on `modulepreload` links — React
emits `fetchPriority="low"` for bootstrap modules — so the only gap was the
public API not forwarding the option. Forward `fetchPriority` from
`preloadModule`'s options to the host dispatcher. The client, Fizz, and
Flight paths already spread the impl options onto the generated link, so no
further plumbing is required.']

["[Fizz] Remove the AbortSignal listener when the stream finishes

renderToReadableStream/resume add an 'abort' listener to options.signal that
only removes itself when the abort fires. When the stream finishes normally or
errors instead, the listener stays attached, so a long-lived or shared signal
accumulates one listener per request and leaks memory.

Remove the listener from the onAllReady/onFatalError resolvers via a hoisted
idempotent helper, so the caller-visible allReady rejection behavior (including
unhandled-rejection diagnostics) is unchanged. The abort path runs the same
helper. Covers the Browser, Edge, Node and Bun web-stream builds."]

['[Perf Tracks] Don't crash on cross-origin Window props

The DEV component performance-track logger reads `$$typeof` and uses `key in
obj` while diffing a component's props. For a cross-origin Window prop these
accesses throw a SecurityError -- the `in` operator triggers [[HasProperty]],
which is blocked for an opaque-origin Window -- and the throw escapes the
commit phase (commitPassiveMountOnFiber) and corrupts the work-in-progress
fiber, freezing the UI with "Should not already be working".

Guard the `$$typeof` read in readReactElementTypeof and the `key in next` /
`key in prev` diff checks through a new safeHas helper, treating an unreadable
value as a non-element and an unreadable key as absent so the DEV-only logger
never throws. A cross-origin Window also exposes child frames as enumerable
numeric indices, so the diff's `in` checks are reached for those too.']

['Support null-prototype objects in Flight

## Motivation

Flight currently rejects objects with a null prototype. That prevents passing map-like records that are intentionally protected from prototype pollution, even though their enumerable string keys are otherwise serializable data.

This is useful for data stores such as graphql-js response stores where keys are not necessarily controlled. The change serializes null-prototype objects with a dedicated Flight marker and revives them with Object.create(null), while preserving keys such as __proto__ as own data properties.

The downstream GraphQL incremental delivery example now has the problem and fix split across two PRs. yaacovCR/graphql-incremental-delivery-client-examples#1 captured the failing RSC boundary after passing the raw GraphQL.js execution result through a Client Component prop. yaacovCR/graphql-incremental-delivery-client-examples#2 applies this React change as a local react-server-dom-webpack patch and flips that boundary test to prove the raw null-prototype product.summary object serializes successfully.

## How did you test this change?

- yarn prettier
- yarn linc
- yarn flow dom-node-webpack
- yarn test ReactFlight-test ReactFlightDOMReply-test
- yarn test --prod ReactFlight-test ReactFlightDOMReply-test --testNamePattern="null-prototype"

I also ran yarn test --prod ReactFlight-test ReactFlightDOMReply-test. The new null-prototype tests passed, but the full production run still failed in existing ReactFlight production error stack expectations unrelated to this change.

Beyond the React unit tests, yaacovCR/graphql-incremental-delivery-client-examples#2 demonstrates the fix in a real react-server-dom-webpack Server-to-Client serialization path: its fixture calls renderToReadableStream with GraphQL.js's raw null-prototype summary object, asserts that React reports no serialization errors, and checks that the emitted Flight payload includes the null-prototype marker. That example PR was verified with npm run verify.', 'simplify', "don't lie", "Merge branch 'main' into null-prototype", "Merge branch 'main' into null-prototype"]

['[rust-compiler] Demote type-only TS/Flow declarations to Unknown passthrough

The 17 TS/Flow type-only declarations (TSTypeAliasDeclaration, TypeAlias,
Declare*, etc.) are never structurally inspected by the compiler: it skips
them in function bodies and preserves them verbatim at module level. Modeling
them as typed Statement variants only added clone/serialize/deserialize code
to the large Statement enum. Demote them to Statement::Unknown (RawNode
passthrough), dispatched at lowering via the new is_type_only_statement_tag.
Their structs remain for the Declaration enum (export type/interface).

Shrinks the release napi binary by ~191KB (7,227,376 -> 7,036,576 bytes).
Behavior is unchanged: the Rust-vs-TS port suite, AST round-trip, and unit
tests all pass.']

["[ReactDOM] Don't crash on bigint values for numeric attributes

isNaN() throws a TypeError on a BigInt, so passing a bigint to one of the
numeric HTML attributes (cols/rows/size/span/rowSpan/start) crashed the
render. Every other attribute already coerces a bigint to a string (the
generic setValueForAttribute path), so this was an inconsistency.

Treat a bigint as a valid numeric value (it is never NaN) across the three
places these attributes are handled: client setProp, hydration property
diffing, and Fizz server rendering. The value is then coerced to a string
like any other attribute."]

['[EnterLeaveEventPlugin] Create enter/leave events lazily

EnterLeaveEventPlugin.extractEvents constructed the mouseleave/mouseenter (or
pointerleave/pointerenter) SyntheticEvent objects up front, before
accumulateEnterLeaveTwoPhaseListeners walked the path to check whether any
matching listener exists. mouseout/mouseover fire on every element-boundary
crossing, so apps with no enter/leave listeners on the path allocated one or
two SyntheticEvent objects per crossing only to discard them.

Defer construction behind factory callbacks, matching the "create event
lazily" pattern already used by SimpleEventPlugin and ScrollEndEventPlugin:
accumulateEnterLeaveListenersForEvent now takes the registration name and a
factory, and builds the event only when listeners.length !== 0.']

['[Flight] Resolve models before JSON.stringify

JSON.stringify's replacer calls back into JS for every property. Do the Flight-specific model resolution in a recursive JS walk first, then stringify the resolved model without a replacer.

Add a Flight test covering an own "__proto__" property nested among sibling properties.']

['[Flight] Preserve debug stacks for specialized Fizz abort errors

During a Fizz prerender, tasks blocked on RSC models should be able to report a Flight-specific abort reason without giving up the high-quality component and owner stacks associated with the model. Today those stacks are recovered by allowing the Flight models and their debug information to resolve, then carefully timing the Fizz abort so the newly available models enrich the aborted tasks without being rendered. Fizz now supports specializing the abort reason for each blocked task, which lets a slot identify that it was blocked on Flight rather than only receiving the request-wide Fizz abort reason. However, triggering that specialization requires erroring the Flight stream, and the error previously rejected the models before their remaining debug information could resolve, losing the improved stacks.

To resolve this conflict, take advantage of the separate debug stream. When one is present, the content stream can be errored with a Flight-specific reason before the Fizz abort while additional debug information continues to be processed through the alternate stream.

Today debug rows are linked to content models by D attachment rows emitted on the content stream. This placement has semantic implications because models block on their debug information, so simply moving those rows to the debug stream would change ordering and would no longer match the behavior of a response without a separate debug stream. Instead, also emit backup attachment rows on the debug stream. During normal rendering these backups are ignored after matching them against the authoritative D rows, with unmatched backups buffered if they arrive first. When the content stream errors, switch to treating the remaining and subsequently arriving backup rows as authoritative for associating additional debug information with each errored chunk.

Preserve the original Flight error and ignore model rows targeting already settled non-stream chunks so a surviving transport can continue processing debug information. Apply the consumer end-time cutoff both when a chunk transitions to errored and whenever pending or late debug models resolve. Truncate shared debug-info arrays in place because Fizz may already hold them through a suspended Lazy, and prevent later rows or pending references from repopulating an array after it has been pruned. This lets every Flight-blocked Fizz task report the specialized Flight error with debug information from before the cutoff.

This currently requires synchronously flushing the relevant Node debug-stream chunks before calling the Fizz abort because Fizz enriches aborted task stacks synchronously during that call. A future change can make this enrichment asynchronous so debug information delivered in microtasks can participate, enabling the same behavior for Web Streams that cannot be flushed synchronously.

This enrichment is enabled only when a separate readable debug stream is present. With a single stream, an error cannot both signal that pending content should be abandoned and leave a transport through which additional debug information can arrive. We explored adding a Flight client abort API to separate those signals, but its practical purpose here would be DEV-only debug enrichment and it would add a second way to indicate that the client may abandon the response, so the additional API surface did not seem justified.

Add Node coverage for real-timer scheduling, debug rows arriving before and after the content error, parsed and pending end-time pruning, replay across multiple errored chunks, preservation of the Flight transport error, distinct Fizz owner stacks, and exact source locations.']

['fix: remove AbortSignal listener on completion to prevent memory leak in Fizz server

When rendering completes (onAllReady) or fails fatally (onFatalError or onShellError),
the abort event listener added to the provided AbortSignal was never removed.
This caused a memory leak because the signal held a reference to the listener
closure, which in turn held a reference to the entire Fizz request object.

Fix: introduce a cleanupSignalListener callback in each render function that
is called from all terminal states (onAllReady, onFatalError, onShellError) to
ensure the event listener is always removed when rendering is done.

Affected files:
- ReactDOMFizzServerBrowser.js (renderToReadableStream, resume)
- ReactDOMFizzServerEdge.js (renderToReadableStream, resume)
- ReactDOMFizzServerBun.js (renderToReadableStream)
- ReactDOMFizzServerNode.js (renderToReadableStream, resume)
- ReactDOMFizzStaticBrowser.js (prerender, resumeAndPrerender)
- ReactDOMFizzStaticEdge.js (prerender, resumeAndPrerender)
- ReactDOMFizzStaticNode.js (prerenderToNodeStream, prerender, resumeAndPrerenderToNodeStream, resumeAndPrerender)

Fixes #36763', 'test: assert no listener accumulation across multiple renders sharing AbortSignal']

['[Fizz] Finalize postponed nextSegmentId after the prelude flush

getPostponedState snapshots request.nextSegmentId at onAllReady, before
the (pull-driven) prelude flush runs. When the shell flush outlines a
completed boundary it advances request.nextSegmentId past that snapshot
(boundary.rootSegmentID = request.nextSegmentId++). The resume seeds its
counter from the snapshot, so it re-allocates segment ids the shell
already emitted. Once the shell and resume are concatenated into one
document there are duplicate <template id="B:n"> / <div hidden id="S:n">
ids, and $RC/$RS resolve via getElementById (first match) -- cross-wiring
boundary content.

This only surfaces when the shell actually outlines a completed boundary
(large shell, suspensey content, or a deferred boundary); with small,
fully-inlined shells the snapshot already equals the final id, which is
why it has been latent.

Hold a reference to the PostponedState that getPostponedState returns
(real-shell case only) and re-sync its nextSegmentId from
flushCompletedQueues once the flush has run, so the resume always
allocates ids strictly above the shell's. nextSegmentId only increases,
so the update is monotonic and idempotent across flush passes; it stays
null for live renders and resumes.', "[Fizz] Avoid for-of in the segment-id regression test

React's lint forbids for..of (no-for-of-loops); the test added in the
previous commit used it to scan emitted ids. Switch both scans to
Array.from(...).forEach(...). No behavior change.", '[Fizz] Assert the segment-id regression on rendered output, not ids

Per review, the regression test asserted on raw B:/S: segment ids, which are
implementation details. Rewrite it to run the prerendered shell and the resume
as a single served document and execute the completion instructions, then
assert the resumed boundary reveals its own content via getVisibleChildren.

The observable symptom of the id collision is that the resumed boundary's $RC
resolves to the shell's element (getElementById, first match) and the boundary
stays on its fallback. Without the fix the test fails with the boundary stuck
on "LoadingC"; with it, both boundaries render their content. No assertions on
segment/boundary ids.', "Cleanup and TODO

Ensuring postponed state isn't accessed too early is potentially disruptive.
We're landing a fix now for API consumers that already do the right thing.
But we should consider reworking the API to make these mistakes impossible."]

["[rust-compiler] Fix has_local_binding for e2e path where component_scope == program_scope

["Fix: use setTimeout fallback for Suspense reveals in hidden/occluded tabs

requestAnimationFrame never fires in hidden or occluded tabs (e.g. background
browser tabs, Chromium window-occlusion on Windows), causing streamed Suspense
boundaries to never reveal or hydrate.

This manifests as two scenarios:
- Scenario A: page opened in background tab. $RT is never set because the
  shell-time rAF never fires, so completeBoundary schedules reveal with rAF
  which also never fires. Boundaries remain forever in $~ (queued) state.
- Scenario B: tab visible at shell paint then hidden. Content moves into the
  DOM via setTimeout, but revealCompletedBoundaries calls
  requestAnimationFrame(_reactRetry) which never fires. Page looks complete
  but buttons are dead (no hydration).

Fix: replace requestAnimationFrame with a scheduleCallback() helper that falls
back to setTimeout(fn, 0) when document.visibilityState === 'hidden'. Also
add a visibilitychange listener (registered once as $RVL) that flushes any
pending $RB entries when the tab becomes visible, covering the race where rAF
was scheduled while the tab was visible but the tab went hidden before it fired.

Also fix the shell-time $RT initialization in ReactDOMFizzInlineShellTime.js
and ReactDOMFizzInstructionSetExternalRuntime.js to eagerly set $RT when the
document is hidden, so completeBoundary uses the setTimeout flush path.

Regenerate ReactDOMFizzInstructionSetInlineCodeStrings.js with
yarn generate-inline-fizz-runtime.

Fixes https://github.com/facebook/react/issues/36741", "[Fizz] Fix hidden-tab Suspense reveals: init $RT synchronously + visibilitychange fallback

Per @eps1lon feedback: narrow the fix to the $RT uninitialized case.
- When document.visibilityState === 'hidden' at shell parse time, initialize
  $RT immediately via performance.now() so completeBoundary takes the
  existing setTimeout flush path (which works in hidden tabs).
- Add a one-shot visibilitychange handler as a fallback for Case B: the tab
  goes hidden after shell load but before the rAF fires, leaving $RT unset.
  The handler initializes $RT at that point, before completeBoundary runs.
- Remove the scheduleCallback() wrapper; restore requestAnimationFrame()
  calls in revealCompletedBoundaries and the rAF-not-yet-fired branch of
  completeBoundary. With $RT guaranteed to be a number, the existing
  setTimeout path handles hidden-tab reveals correctly.
- Keep the visibilitychange flush listener in completeBoundary that calls
  $RV($RB) when the tab becomes visible, ensuring any boundaries queued
  while hidden are revealed once the tab is foregrounded.

Closes #36741"]
